rows.requirement: Platforms MUST render all top-level totals entries in the order provided
rows.quote: Platforms MUST render all top-level entries in the order provided:
rows.notes: Rendering/display behavior; not enforceable by JSON Schema.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST NOT interpret, filter, reorder, aggregate, or apply their own display logic to totals
rows.quote:
Platforms MUST NOT interpret, filter, reorder, aggregate, or apply display
logic of their own.

rows.requirement: Unknown (non-well-known) totals types MUST include display_text
rows.notes: Schema-enforced via totals.json if/then: type not in well-known enum -> require display_text.

rows.requirement: Exactly one totals entry of type subtotal MUST be present
rows.notes: Schema-enforced: source/schemas/shopping/types/totals.json contains subtotal minContains:1 maxContains:1.

rows.requirement: Exactly one totals entry of type total MUST be present
rows.notes: Schema-enforced: totals.json contains total minContains:1 maxContains:1.

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST NOT substitute their own computed totals for the business values
rows.quote:
Platforms MUST NOT substitute their own computed totals for the business's
values.

rows.requirement: Platforms MAY verify sum of non-total entry amounts equals the total entry amount
rows.quote: Platforms MAY verify the provided totals:
rows.notes: PROSE-ONLY arithmetic invariant; verification is MAY/optional and NOT in totals.json. We implement sum(non-total)==total as a coded check (AMB-004).

rows.requirement: Platforms MUST NOT autonomously complete a checkout with mismatched totals
rows.quote:
platforms MUST NOT autonomously complete
a checkout with mismatched totals.
rows.notes: Behavioral; depends on the optional sum check.

rows.requirement: On mismatched totals, platforms SHOULD reject the checkout or escalate for buyer review via continue_url
rows.quote:
Platforms SHOULD reject the checkout or
escalate and ask for buyer review via `continue_url`.

rows.requirement: When display_text is provided, platforms MUST use it
rows.quote: When `display_text` is provided, platforms MUST use it.

rows.requirement: Subtractive well-known types (discount, items_discount) MUST have negative amounts
rows.quote:
subtractive types
(discount, items_discount) MUST have negative amounts
rows.notes: Schema strictly-negative: source/schemas/shopping/types/total.json if type in [discount, items_discount] then amount exclusiveMaximum:0.

rows.requirement: Additive well-known types (subtotal, fulfillment, tax, fee) MUST have non-negative amounts
rows.quote:
additive types
(subtotal, fulfillment, tax, fee) MUST have non-negative amounts.
rows.notes: Schema non-negative: total.json if type in [subtotal, fulfillment, tax, fee] then amount minimum:0.

rows.requirement: Sum of a parent entry lines[] amounts MUST equal the parent entry amount
rows.notes: PROSE-ONLY arithmetic invariant; no JSON-Schema construct enforces it. We implement as a coded check (AMB-004).

rows.requirement: Platforms SHOULD render sub-lines when provided
rows.quote:
Platforms SHOULD render sub-lines
when provided.

rows.requirement: Each sub-line entry requires both display_text and amount
Each top-level totals entry requires both type and amount
rows.notes: Schema-required fields on each total entry.

rows.requirement: Price requires a non-negative integer amount and an ISO 4217 currency code
rows.notes: price.json amount $ref amount.json (integer minimum:0); currency pattern ^[A-Z]{3}$. Distinct from signed_amount used by totals entries which permits negatives.
